meaning: adj.无重负的,不被认为重要的,[统]未加权的
examples: The simple unweighted average of countries' growth rates was virtually identical in Africa and Asia.
By default, the calculation is linear and unweighted, but quadratic and weighted variants are also provided.
This problem is a generalization of the problem of finding the maximum cardinality clique of an unweighted graph.
These tariffs have to be cut by an unweighted average of 36% between 1995 and 2000.
For domestic actively managed funds the (unweighted) average annual expense ratio has dipped from 1.56% of assets in 2003 to 1.45% today.
